# Naser Al-Rais

> Final-year undergraduate at the University of Cambridge (BA Genetics) researching how brain ageing and cognitive decline can be reversed.

Naser Al-Rais (Nas) is a Cambridge genetics student and early-career researcher focused on the molecular biology of ageing, neurodegeneration, and epigenetic rejuvenation. His work spans wet-lab biophysics, computational biology, and science policy.

## Research

- **Church Lab, Wyss Institute / Harvard Medical School** (Jun–Sep 2026): Rejuvenating dopaminergic neurons as a therapeutic strategy for Parkinson's disease via partial reprogramming — resetting the epigenome to a youthful state using transcription factors.
- **Knowles Lab, University of Cambridge** (Oct 2025–Apr 2026): Research assistant investigating disordered proteins, liquid–liquid phase separation, and the effects of small molecules; built microfluidic devices in-house.
- **Lu Lab, Harvard Medical School** (Jun–Sep 2025): Engineered a novel inducible protein aggregation model; studied toxicity and clearance of protein aggregates.

## Writing & Policy

- "What is the cost of saying no to first-generation Alzheimer's therapies?" (May 2026) — essay on NICE, lecanemab, unpaid care, and the economics of drug approvals.
- Fellow, No Patient Left Behind (pharmaceutical innovation advocacy).

## Full Content

- Full essay text: /llms-full.txt
